title: Stuffed Capsicum (Bharwa Shimla Mirch)

ingredients.name: medium capsicum (bell peppers), tops cut and deseeded

subs.name: paneer crumbled or a potato-paneer mix for a richer filling
a squeeze of lemon juice into the filling

steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a small pan. Add cumin seeds, then the chopped onion and green chilli. Cook until the onion is soft, about 3 minutes. Add ginger and fry for 30 seconds more.

Add the mashed potato, boiled peas, red chilli powder, amchur and garam masala. Mix well and cook for 2 minutes. Season with salt. Stir in coriander leaves. The filling should hold its shape when pressed. Set aside to cool slightly.
Rub the inside of each hollowed capsicum with a pinch of salt. Fill each one firmly with the potato-pea mixture, packing it to the top. Replace the cut tops as lids if desired.

Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wide, heavy kadai or non-stick pan over medium flame. Place the stuffed capsicums upright in the pan. Cover and cook for 8 minutes, turning them every 2 minutes so all sides get colour.
Remove the lid, increase the heat slightly and cook for 3 more minutes until the skin blisters and darkens in patches. The capsicum should be soft but still hold its shape.

Arrange on a plate, garnish with a few coriander leaves, and serve alongside phulka or plain rice and dal. The sweet-spicy contrast of the pepper skin and savoury potato filling is the appeal of this dish.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: potato → paneer crumbled or a potato-paneer mix for a richer filling (classic premium variation); peas → corn kernels; amchur → a squeeze of lemon juice into the filling
